channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Bungie Confirm First Marathon Esports Tournament One Week After Release
item.content:encoded:
Marathon, the new first-person shooter by Bungie, has confirmed its very first esports tournament, the ‘Tau Ceti Cup’ just a week after the game’s release.

Bungie had not previously confirmed any plans to push its new extraction shooter as an esport, though the multiplayer game does have a ranked mode and competitive interest. However, the Tau Ceti Cup is more of a showmatch featuring popular streamers, rather than a sporting event to determine the world’s best Marathon team.

The inaugural Marathon tournament will feature six teams of content creators, including the likes of BurntPeanut, Shroud, and Aztecross, competing on Saturday, March 14.

The winning team will earn custom-made Marathon Jackets made by Australian cosplayer Vamprea, as well as rewards for their community of viewers.

Bungie is also hosting a similar event for Australian creators only, called the Marathon Australia Invitational, with the winning team of the invite-only event receiving the same custom jackets and the game’s collector’s edition.

Maraton Tau Ceti Cup Teams

Team BurntPeanut

  • TheBurntPeanut
  • Gingy
  • HutchMF

Team Aztecross

  • Aztecross
  • Symfuhny
  • Trey24k

Team Shroud

  • Shroud
  • LVNDMARK
  • Just9n

Team Bearki

  • Bearki
  • DeadlySlob
  • Aspen

Team Jour_du_Grenier

  • Jour_du_Grenier
  • Tonto
  • Shisheyu

Team Rubius

  • Rubius
  • IloJuan
  • TheGrefg

Marathon Tau Ceti Cup Twitch Drops

Viewers who watch any streamer’s point of view of the Marathon Tau Ceti Cup will be able to earn rewards for the game through Twitch drops.

There are three Angry Bunny-themed rewards available for watching the event. A profile emblem after one hour of watch-time, a profile background after two hours of watch-time, and a weapon sticker after three hours of watch-time.

To claim these Twitch drops, viewers will need to:

  • Link their Bungie.net account to their Twitch account
  • Watch a Marathon stream of one of the competitors (on Saturday, March 14, between 15:00 EDT/13:00 GMT and 19:00 EDT/15:00 GMT) in the Twitch Directory with Drops enabled during the event
  • Claim each reward from their Twitch Inventory, then log in to Marathon to receive them

Do you think Marathon has potential as an esport? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord

And find out all the things you should do first on Marathon, before you try to become a pro player.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Steam Machine Targets 1080p at 30 FPS for Deck Verified Games
item.content:encoded:
All Steam Deck games on the Steam Machine will target 1080p at 30 FPS.

Late last year, Valve announced its return to the console business with its next-gen Steam Machine. The upcoming system is capable of playing at 4K resolution at 60 FPS on a “semi-custom” AMD CPU and GPU. Now, performance details for the Steam Deck-verified games have been revealed at GDC.

All Steam Deck-Verified Games are Steam Machine Verified

As spotted by PC Gamer, Valve provided new details on Steam hardware at GDC 2026, talking about SteamOS, Deck, Frame, and Machine. On the Steam Machine Verified slide, it was mentioned that Deck-Verified games are verified for the consoles. Players will receive the same input expectations for Steam Deck. The console will target 30FPS at 1080p for Deck Verified games, and performance on the Steam machine will be 6x that of the Steam Deck. Additionally, it doesn’t test display resolution for legibility and has an updated API to detect hardware.

Regarding Steam Frame verification, there is no verified program for streaming. If it runs well on your host PC, then it should be okay for Steam Frame. Steam Frame verified is for standalone play only; VR and non-VR games are being tested. Coming to performance, standalone VR titles will target 90 FPS whereas standalone 2D titles will target 30 FPS at 1280×720.

In other news, Valve has issued a lengthy statement responding to New York’s lawsuit . Additionally, Xbox has announced that developers will receive devkits, or ‘alpha versions’ of Project Helix, next year. What are your thoughts on Steam Machine targeting 1080p at 30 FPS for Deck Verified Games? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Xbox teasing the return of iconic titles with new ways to play. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Xbox Teases Return of ‘Iconic Games’ With ‘New Ways to Play’
item.content:encoded:
During the Xbox Developer Summit at GDC 2026, the Xbox Game Preservation team teased the return of ‘iconic games’ with new ways to play.

Xbox came in guns blazing at GDC 2026, talking about the company’s next generation. The VP of Next-Gen also announced that developers will receive devkits, or ‘alpha versions’ of Project Helix, next year, which gives us some idea of when to expect the next Xbox console.

Xbox Game Preservation Team Confirms Classic Games Are Returning

Speaking at GDC, Xbox confirmed its commitment to keeping four generations of games playable for years to come. During the 25th Anniversary, the Game Preservation team will be “rolling out new ways to play some of the most iconic games from our past.” During the panel, eagle-eyed players spotted games like Fuzion Frenzy, Panzer Dragon, and more being teased.

The team also spoke about “breaking down the barriers between console and PC games for more seamless cross-device play.” The Xbox experience is also becoming more consistent, which will help provide developers “a more unified path to reach more players while helping reduce development costs.”

Additionally, all that Xbox knows about “building a leading gaming OS” is being brought to Windows this April. An Xbox Mode will be introduced for Windows 11, allowing you to seamlessly switch between productivity and play with a familiar full-screen, controller-optimized Xbox experience while embracing the openness of Windows.

Xbox also mentioned the “return of iconic first-party franchises like Halo and Gears of War” this year, along with titles from their partners, including Beethoven & Dinosaur’s Mixtape, Crimson Desert from Pearl Abyss, and more.

In other news, Satya Nadella has insisted that Microsoft will always invest in gaming. Additionally, check out the Xbox Game Pass Wave 1 titles for March 2026. What are your thoughts on Xbox teasing the return of iconic games. What are your thoughts on Xbox teasing the return of iconic games? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Sony officially confirming the PlayStation Plus Game Catalog titles for March 2026. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
